assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can install CLI tools if a CLI-heavy route is selected.
No Neon account, project, production branch, or development branch exists yet.
No Neon getting-started product path is preselected.

developer_goal: Create a Neon account and complete the earliest official getting-started first-success outcome for Neon Postgres.

first_success.outcome: Depends on the still-unresolved getting-started route: either Schema Diff shows identical development and production branches after the Console tour reset, or an init/backend quickstart reaches its own documented terminal.
first_success.observable_signal: For the Tour the Neon Console route, Schema Diff reports development and production are identical after the final reset.

route_selection.surface: Neon Console and Start with Neon documentation
route_selection.rule: Current Neon docs home presents multiple first routes; select only after one complete official path is fixed. Equal consequential routes remain unresolved.

candidates.name: Tour the Neon Console (formerly Learn the basics / signing-up)
candidates.reason: Still the detailed Console tour ending in branching and Schema Diff, but it now also inserts Managed Better Auth and early-exit links to connect-your-stack, so required scope is ambiguous.

candidates.name: One-command setup via npx neon@latest init
candidates.reason: Neon docs home now leads with AI-guided one-command setup that creates a project, applies schema, and writes .env; that is a different surface and terminal from the Console tour.

candidates.name: Full backend quickstart (Next.js + Neon backend)
candidates.reason: Docs home presents a full backend tutorial as a primary getting-started path with region constraints and neon.ts deploy steps.

candidates.name: Connect Neon to your stack after minimal onboarding
candidates.reason: The Console tour explicitly offers an early exit to connecting tools before the branching exercises; that is a shorter alternate goal, not the full tour terminal.

required_path.action: Sign up for Neon with email, GitHub, Google, or another supported partner account.

required_fields.label: Supported signup identity
required_fields.notes: Docs list email, GitHub, Google, or partner account. The public signup page fetch did not expose concrete email/password field controls.

required_path.observable_result: The developer is authenticated and enters Neon Console onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete Neon Console onboarding to create a Project with a production branch.

required_fields.label: Project onboarding selections
required_fields.notes: Tour calls onboarding self-explanatory. Separate project-create docs list Project Name, Postgres version, Cloud provider, and Region for additional projects, but first-project onboarding fields remain unenumerated.

required_path.observable_result: A Project Dashboard opens with production and neondb available.

required_path.action: If the Console tour route is selected, run the SQL Editor sample table, inspect Tables, create a development branch, and complete CLI or psql branching exercises through Schema Diff reset.

required_fields.label: development branch name and production parent
Neon CLI install, neon auth, and branch/schema commands
required_fields.notes: Full branching half of the fetched page was truncated; exact remaining CLI and Schema Diff steps need a complete page or walkthrough.

required_path.observable_result: The selected getting-started route's terminal signal is visible.

prerequisites.description: For the Console tour branching half, Homebrew or npm for Neon CLI and a psql client when following the psql modification steps.

external_gates.description: GitHub, Google, or partner identity authorization when those signup methods are chosen.
Browser-based Neon CLI authorization during neon auth.

unavoidable_waits.description: Neon must provision the project, production compute, and later development branch before SQL and Schema Diff steps can succeed.

platform_outcomes.description: Onboarding creates a Project with a production branch, default database, role, and compute.
Creating a development branch yields an isolated copy of production for schema experiments.

excluded.item: Reading Neon documentation home as a counted action
excluded.reason: Docs navigation is evidence for route selection, not a product interaction.
Tour presents inviting members as optional team guidance.

excluded.item: AI Assistant natural-language prompts in the SQL Editor
excluded.reason: Tour presents Try the AI Assistant as exploratory practice after the sample table; it is not required to create a development branch or reach Schema Diff.

excluded.item: Managed Better Auth enablement during the Console tour
excluded.reason: Tour includes a Managed Better Auth section, but branching and Schema Diff do not depend on enabling Auth.

sources.title: Neon documentation
evidence_supported: Tour and connect guides as alternate getting-started entries

sources.title: Tour the Neon Console
evidence_supported: Create development branch and CLI/psql branching direction

sources.title: Connect with psql
evidence_supported: psql connection and platform-specific install guidance referenced by the tour

sources.title: Neon Console signup
evidence_supported: Concrete email or OAuth field controls were not extractable from the public fetch

sources.title: Manage projects
evidence_supported: Additional project create fields: name, Postgres version, cloud provider, region

uncertainties.question: Which current Neon getting-started route is the canonical shortest path: Console tour, npx neon init, or full backend quickstart?
uncertainties.impact: These routes diverge in required CLI work, Auth setup, framework scaffolding, and first-success signal.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A bounded goal choice, then a complete fresh-account walkthrough of that single official route.

uncertainties.question: Which exact fields appear on Neon signup and first-project onboarding?
uncertainties.impact: Signup and onboarding fields change required-action and required-field counts for the Console tour.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated screenshots of console.neon.tech/signup and first-project onboarding for a fresh account.

uncertainties.question: Which OS path applies for psql install if the selected Console tour branch requires psql?
uncertainties.impact: psql install commands differ by OS and architecture and change implementation steps.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Pin one OS for any psql-required branch of the path, or confirm the CLI-only branch covers Schema Diff without psql.
